name: John Cornelius
title: Operations & Continuous Improvement Leader
summary: Operations and continuous improvement leader with manufacturing progression across quality/materials, operations management, and enterprise AI/Six Sigma work. Supports 9 facilities, manages a 40+ project improvement portfolio tied to $4.2M+ in annualized savings, and turns plant priorities into daily management routines, coached problem solving, and measurable safety, quality, delivery, and cost results. Retired Army aviation officer background adds disciplined standards, risk management, and leader development.

Operations Management: Plant operating cadence and KPI reviews
Production, maintenance, material flow, quality, and safety coordination
Continuous Improvement: TPM, Hoshin Kanri, standard work, and root-cause analysis
Rapid Improvement Events and benefits tracking
OEE, scrap, downtime, safety, and cost improvement
Analytics & Practical AI: Power BI, Python, SQL, MES, ERP, and SCADA data
AI-assisted document review and vector retrieval
Operational decision support for plant leaders
Program Execution: Change adoption with operators and supervisors
Leadership: Army aviation standards and risk management

experience.title: AI & Six Sigma Lead / Operations & Quality Leader
duties: Progressed through Quality & Raw Materials Manager, Operations Manager, and AI & Six Sigma Lead roles, expanding from plant quality/materials execution into operations cadence and multi-site CI/analytics support.
Ran or supported daily management routines across production priorities, maintenance constraints, material flow, quality, safety, staffing coverage, and shift-level execution.
Support process optimization, CI governance, and analytics-enabled improvement across 9 manufacturing facilities.
Build Power BI and Python analytics workflows for loss analysis, SPC, project tracking, and operational decision support.
Translate technical and analytical work into clear business language for plant managers, functional leaders, and senior stakeholders.
achievements: Manage a 40+ project DMAIC and continuous-improvement portfolio tied to $4.2M+ in annualized savings.
Reduced blowdown oil waste by 70.8% using BrainCube analytics and airflow optimization, delivering $189K in verified annual savings.
Delivered $37K in verified annual savings through AI-based PID auto-tuning deployed in Python/Jupyter Notebook.
Eliminated 15-minute pre-shift handovers by building an automated Power Apps shift brief system, saving $16.8K per year.
Cut JHA review time by 75% using a ChatGPT API workflow with company-specific vector retrieval.
Train and coach 60+ Green Belts and analysts on structured problem solving, data analysis, practical AI tools, and business-case communication.

experience.title: Army Aviation Operations & Standardization Leader (Retired CW3)
duties: Completed a 20-year Army career with UH-60/MH-60 aviation, instructor pilot, and operations leadership experience.
Led aviation operations and readiness programs involving 30-250+ personnel, equipment accountability, logistics, maintenance coordination, and safety-critical execution.
Managed operating requirements involving $10M+ budgets, aircraft readiness, training resources, scheduling constraints, and risk controls.
Built training, evaluation, standards, and risk-management systems that improved readiness, accountability, and disciplined execution.
achievements: Designed and executed learning and development programs that progressed 55 pilots to mission-qualified status.
Mentored 25 pilots to pilot-in-command status while conducting standardization evaluations.
Managed aircrew training and Electronic Flight Bag programs, maintaining current DoD flight information and digital procedures.
Led revision of USAACE Regulation 350-3, consolidating two regulations and streamlining aircraft scheduling.

education.degree: M.S. - Unmanned Systems, Human Factors Engineering
education.details: GPA 4.0; human-machine integration, decision-making, and safety for unmanned systems.
Technical foundation in systems, safety, and logistics for aviation program oversight.

affiliations: Civil Air Patrol - sUAS training and community programs
U.S. Army veteran; community volunteer leadership in Eastern Panhandle, WV
